---

## TWEET 1 (Storyworth price comparison)

Storyworth charges $99/year to email your grandparents interview questions.

We built a 50-page workbook with 47 interview questions + family timeline + photo guide + writing prompts for $17. One time. No subscription.

The family history space is ripe.

---

## TWEET 2 (Religious streak app gap)

No one has built denomination-specific habit apps.

Hallow serves Catholics. YouVersion serves generic Christians. Muslim Pro serves Muslims.

Zero apps for Sikh daily practice. Zero for Torah reading streaks. Zero for Buddhist meditation streaks.

We built 13 of them.

---

## TWEET 3 (Habit app revenue proof)

HabitKit: $15K/month. Generic habit tracker. No AI. No celebrity endorsements. No funding.

Just a clean streak mechanic and $7.99/month subscription.

The niche faith/spiritual version of this does not exist. We're building it.

---

## THREAD (Claude Code suite positioning)

Claude Code for Designers sells on Gumroad for an unknown price.

It's the only Claude Code product on Gumroad.

We have 8.

1/ Here's what competitors are missing about the Claude Code digital product space:

2/ There are 10,000+ Claude Code users who have no idea how to use it for their specific use case. Designers. Solopreneurs. Non-technical founders. Content creators. That's 4 different $47 products.

3/ Nobody has bundled these into a suite. The "learn Claude Code" market is fragmented. Every product is standalone. The gap: a $97-197 bundle for the full system.

4/ We're building the suite. Currently: 8 products targeting every non-technical audience. Combined unbundled value: $376. Bundle: $97.

5/ The market doesn't have a comprehensive Claude Code learning suite. We're making it.

Building in public. Day 44.
